files_changed.summary: Added loadLayout import, static layoutEditorConfig, this.load.json in preload(), this.layout = loadLayout() at top of create(), replaced all literal coordinates and sizes with this.layout.* references.
Created layout JSON with 6 objects: marker_circle, marker_label, marker_prompt, marker_quest_indicator, progress_bar, map_label.

layout_objects.name: marker_circle
layout_objects.source: this.add.circle(x, y, 22, ...) + .setStrokeStyle(3, ...) — uniform across all location markers in per-location loop

layout_objects.name: marker_label
layout_objects.source: y + 30 — label text y offset relative to marker center in per-location loop

layout_objects.name: marker_prompt
layout_objects.source: y - 34 — enter/lock prompt y offset (same literal in both unlocked and locked branches)

layout_objects.name: marker_quest_indicator
layout_objects.source: x + 18, y - 18 — quest exclamation mark offset relative to marker center

layout_objects.name: progress_bar
layout_objects.source: barW = 40, height = 4, y + 42 — progress bar dimensions and y offset for locked markers

layout_objects.name: map_label
layout_objects.source: WORLD.width / 2 → 768, WORLD.height - 14 → 1010 — static scene label at bottom-center

positions_left_in_code.note: ENTER_RADIUS is a gameplay interaction radius used in proximity checks, not a visual position. Left as named constant per dispatch spec which explicitly calls it out as EXEMPT.

positions_left_in_code.reason: EXEMPT — tap-to-enter touch radius, game-logic constant
positions_left_in_code.note: The value 40 in _handlePointerDown is a pointer tap radius for entering locations, not a visual position.
350 is a fade-in duration in milliseconds, not a pixel coordinate.

positions_left_in_code.reason: EXEMPT — dynamic positions from registry data
positions_left_in_code.code: WORLD.width / 2, WORLD.height / 2 — map background image centering
positions_left_in_code.note: The world map image position is computed from WORLD constants (already externalized in neighborhoodLayout.js). No raw literal — WORLD.width and WORLD.height are the data constants.

positions_left_in_code.reason: EXEMPT — physics bounds derived entirely from imported constants
positions_left_in_code.note: All four values are BOUNDARY_PADDING and WORLD constants from neighborhoodLayout.js — no raw literals.

architecture_notes: OverworldScene is largely data-driven — marker positions come from getLocalScenes() registry data (sceneDef.worldPos.x/y), not inline literals. Canvas dimensions come from WORLD const in neighborhoodLayout.js.

The 5 extracted loop-relative constants (marker_circle, marker_label, marker_prompt, marker_quest_indicator, progress_bar) are uniform offsets applied to dynamic per-marker positions. They are inside a per-location render loop. Per spec §8, loop-generated positions are exempt; however these are literal constants (not computed) so they were extracted to give the layout editor visibility into marker styling.
The map_label is the only truly static absolute position — resolved from WORLD.width / 2 = 768 and WORLD.height - 14 = 1010.
No static room geometry exists in this scene (no walls, floors, exits). NeighborhoodScene precedent of empty objects:{} would also have been valid.
No bugs found during extraction. The _hasQuestAt logic that always returns true for any quest not in completedQuests (even before being active) was observed but left untouched per Reviewer FAIL rule.

self_audit.all_json_keys_referenced: PASS — all 6 keys confirmed referenced via grep
self_audit.layout_editor_config: PASS — static layoutEditorConfig confirmed at line 34
self_audit.diff_only_positions: PASS — git diff shows only: import, layoutEditorConfig, preload load.json, create loadLayout, literal-to-layout replacements. Zero logic changes.

suggested_next_agent: code-quality-reviewer or another phaser-scene-layout-extractor invocation for the next scene
